arguments.task: Open the official MrScraper documentation and related first-party pages needed to verify: what MrScraper does, documented authentication methods for the API, API surface type (REST/GraphQL/Both/None), any pricing or access-gating information for API usage, and whether there is any first-party mention of an MCP server. Read the actual page content, not snippets. Return the canonical URLs visited and concise extracted evidence quotes for each field. If docs reference pricing or signup pages on the main website, open those official pages too.

raw_result.description: Web scraping platform for automated website data extraction via AI-guided and manual workflows.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official API docs require a MrScraper API token, sent in the `x-api-token` request header; analytics endpoints also document passing the API token as the `apiTokenName` query parameter.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official pricing shows a self-serve Free plan that includes 1,000 API tokens, alongside Standard and Pro plans and pay-as-you-go overages.

api_surface.breadth: Public docs describe a REST API with resource-oriented URLs, JSON responses, standard HTTP verbs/status codes, dedicated authentication docs, and additional MCP server documentation for agent access.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official docs include a cloud-hosted MrScraper MCP server over HTTP that uses a MrScraper API token.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Pricing page ties usage to API-token quotas by plan: Free 1,000 API tokens, Standard 50,000, Pro 200,000, with pay-as-you-go charges after limits; no separate request-per-minute limit was verified from the read pages.
raw_result.notable_signals: First-party docs are clear and product-specific, include dedicated API auth and overview pages, and provide official MCP server documentation, which is a strong agent-enablement signal.
